threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus will arrive on time. Locals know it's a suggestion, not an obligation.
Heard someone on the 36 talking passionately about the best chicken shop in Peckham, then ordered a kebab at 3 AM. Priorities, I guess.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw an old lady at the corner bakery arguing with the staff about the perfect croissant, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find people casually playing giant chess in a park while the S-Bahn rattles above, blending everyday life with quirky pastimes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they confidently navigate the BART delays while sipping on a horchata from a street vendor, no sweat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo helping a lost tourist with Muni directions, all while juggling a cocktail. Only in the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think our parks are just for photos, but locals use them for siestas and lively chess games. It's a different vibe, trust me.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their intense confusion over the 'carrer' signs; they always pause before choosing which way to go.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still carry an umbrella everywhere; we know you haven’t learned yet that you’ll just end up soaked anyway.
Last night on the 35, a bloke dropped a whole kebab on the floor and just kept staring at it like it was a life decision. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the old man selling handmade candles next to the döner kiosk, chatting with the hipsters and locals alike. That blend is Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg have I seen a kebab shop that opens at 3 AM and serves vegan döner. A true late-night lifesaver for the post-club crowd.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they instinctively check BART’s status before heading out, but still end up waiting while a dog disrupts Muni.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo waiting for a Muni bus last night, clutching a bouquet. Maybe he was late to a wedding or just celebrating the weekend. Classic SF!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars close early. But locals know the best ones, like La Madre, don’t really get going until after midnight.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their route to the nearest metro; they’ll always hesitate at the ticket machines and fumble with their change.
